edges.mechanism: FLC is a MADS-box floral repressor; high FLC = late flowering, null flc = early flowering

evidence.title: Regulation of Flowering in Arabidopsis by an FLC Homologue
evidence.authors: Ratcliffe OJ, Nadzan GC, Reuber TL, Riechmann JL
evidence.evidence_sentence: The Arabidopsis FLC gene encodes a MADS domain protein that acts as a repressor of flowering. High levels of FLC transcript are sufficient to produce very late flowering and null flc mutants are early flowering.

edges.mechanism: FCA is an RNA-binding protein regulating alternative RNA 3'-end processing of FLC antisense COOLAIR, promoting chromatin silencing of FLC

evidence.title: Autonomous Pathway: FLOWERING LOCUS C Repression through an Antisense-Mediated Chromatin-Silencing Mechanism
evidence.authors: Wu Z, Fang X, Zhu D, Dean C
evidence.evidence_sentence: FCA is a nuclear RNA-binding protein that functions in regulating alternative RNA 3'-end processing. FCA, FPA, and FY activities promote proximal polyadenylation of COOLAIR.
evidence.claim: FCA represses FLC via COOLAIR processing

edges.mechanism: FPA contains three RNA recognition motifs and promotes proximal polyadenylation of COOLAIR antisense transcript, leading to FLC chromatin silencing

evidence.title: FPA, a Gene Involved in Floral Induction in Arabidopsis, Encodes a Protein Containing RNA-Recognition Motifs
evidence.authors: Schomburg FM, Patton DA, Meinke DW, Amasino RM
evidence.evidence_sentence: The predicted FPA protein contains three RNA recognition motifs in the N-terminal region; autonomous-pathway genes act to decrease FLC.

edges.mechanism: FY (homolog of yeast Pfs2p polyadenylation factor) interacts with FCA via WW domain and promotes proximal polyadenylation of sense and antisense FLC transcripts

evidence.title: Hypomorphic Alleles Reveal FCA-Independent Roles for FY in the Regulation of FLOWERING LOCUS C
evidence.authors: Feng W, Jacob Y, Veley KM, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: FY promotes usage of a proximal polyadenylation site of the sense FLC transcript.

edges.mechanism: FLD is a histone H3K4me2 demethylase (LSD1 homolog) that demethylates the FLC gene body, silencing transcription

evidence.title: Autonomous Pathway: FLOWERING LOCUS C Repression through an Antisense-Mediated Chromatin-Silencing Mechanism
evidence.authors: Wu Z, Fang X, Zhu D, Dean C
evidence.evidence_sentence: FLD is a histone Lys-4 demethylase; FLD demethylates H3K4me2 mainly in the body of the FLC gene.
evidence.claim: FLD represses FLC by H3K4me2 demethylation

edges.mechanism: FVE is a retinoblastoma-associated protein homolog in a histone deacetylase complex; FVE is required for histone deacetylation at FLC chromatin

evidence.title: FVE, an Arabidopsis homologue of the retinoblastoma-associated protein that regulates flowering time and cold response, binds to chromatin as a large multiprotein complex
evidence.authors: Jeon J, Kim J
evidence.evidence_sentence: In the fve mutants, the FLC chromatin was enriched in acetylated histones H3 and H4, thereby indicating that FVE is required for histone deacetylation at the FLC chromatin.
evidence.claim: FVE represses FLC via histone deacetylation

edges.mechanism: LD is a homeodomain-containing transcriptional repressor of FLC; interacts with FLD and SDG26 in autonomous pathway complex

evidence.title: Characterization of an autonomous pathway complex that promotes flowering in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Qi PL, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: LD is a homeodomain-containing protein that functions as a transcriptional repressor of FLC; SDG26 interacts with the autonomous pathway components LD and FLD and thereby forms a complex that promotes flowering by suppressing FLC expression.

edges.mechanism: FLK is a K homology RNA-binding protein that represses FLC expression; possibly transcriptional or RNA-directed chromatin regulation

evidence.title: Research progress on the autonomous flowering time pathway in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Cheng JZ, Zhou YP, Lv TX, Xie CP, Tian CE
evidence.journal: Physiology and Molecular Biology of Plants
evidence.evidence_sentence: FLK may suppress FLC partially at transcriptional level or through RNA-directed chromatin regulation.

edges.mechanism: REF6 is a JMJC-domain histone demethylase acting on FLC antisense RNA to repress FLC

evidence.title: Research progress on the autonomous flowering time pathway in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Cheng JZ, Zhou YP, Lv TX, Xie CP, Tian CE
evidence.journal: Physiology and Molecular Biology of Plants
evidence.evidence_sentence: REF6 was found to act primarily on FLC antisense RNA through its JMJC domain (Jumonji C domain).

edges.mechanism: SDG26 interacts with LD and FLD in the autonomous pathway complex (AuPC) to suppress FLC

evidence.title: Characterization of an autonomous pathway complex that promotes flowering in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Qi PL, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: SDG26 interacts with the autonomous pathway components LD and FLD and thereby forms a complex that promotes flowering by suppressing FLC expression.

edges.mechanism: APRF1 is part of the autonomous pathway complex; aprf1 mutants show weakly delayed flowering, synergistic with sdg26

evidence.title: Characterization of an autonomous pathway complex that promotes flowering in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Qi PL, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: aprf1 mutants show weakly delayed flowering; sdg26 aprf1 double mutant shows further delayed flowering vs single mutants.
evidence.claim: APRF1 represses FLC in autonomous pathway complex

edges.mechanism: HUA2 positively regulates FLC; promotes FLC expression and delayed flowering

evidence.title: Research progress on the autonomous flowering time pathway in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Cheng JZ, Zhou YP, Lv TX, Xie CP, Tian CE
evidence.journal: Physiology and Molecular Biology of Plants
evidence.evidence_sentence: HUA2 has been found to positively regulate FLC expression in the autonomous pathway.

edges.mechanism: PEP (PEPPER), a paralogue of FLK, positively regulates FLC expression; PEP overexpression causes high FLC and late flowering

evidence.title: Research progress on the autonomous flowering time pathway in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Cheng JZ, Zhou YP, Lv TX, Xie CP, Tian CE
evidence.journal: Physiology and Molecular Biology of Plants
evidence.evidence_sentence: PEPPER (PEP) is a paralogue of FLK and positively regulates expression of FLC in Arabidopsis; PEP overexpression resulted in high FLC transcript levels and delayed flowering.

edges.mechanism: SSF (Sister of FCA) is a functionally divergent homolog of FCA; ssf mutants flower early indicating SSF represses flowering

evidence.title: Molecular variation in a functionally divergent homolog of FCA regulates flowering time in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Wang Y, Tao Z, Wang W, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: ssf mutants showed early flowering, demonstrating functional divergence between these FCA homologs despite their structural similarity.

edges.mechanism: PKL (PICKLE) CHD3-type chromatin remodeler contributes to FLC repression in the autonomous pathway

evidence.title: Research progress on the autonomous flowering time pathway in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Cheng JZ, Zhou YP, Lv TX, Xie CP, Tian CE
evidence.journal: Physiology and Molecular Biology of Plants
evidence.evidence_sentence: PKL is associated with autonomous pathway FLC repression via chromatin remodeling.

edges.mechanism: FCA autoregulates itself by promoting proximal polyadenylation within intron 3 of its own pre-mRNA, reducing functional FCA transcript

evidence.title: Autoregulation of FCA pre-mRNA processing controls Arabidopsis flowering time
evidence.authors: Quesada V, Macknight R, Dean C, Simpson GG
evidence.evidence_sentence: FCA negatively regulates its expression by ultimately promoting proximal poly(A) site usage within intron 3 of its own pre-mRNA.

edges.mechanism: FCA physically interacts with FY via WW domain enabling FCA-mediated RNA 3' processing

evidence.title: Autoregulation of FCA pre-mRNA processing controls Arabidopsis flowering time
evidence.authors: Quesada V, Macknight R, Dean C, Simpson GG
evidence.evidence_sentence: FCA interacts with a conserved 3' end processing factor, Pfs2p/FY, through its WW domain and this interaction is genetically required for FCA autoregulation.

edges.mechanism: miR156 targets SPL9 transcript by cleavage/translational repression

evidence.title: The sequential action of miR156 and miR172 regulates developmental timing in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Wu G, Park MY, Conway SR, Wang JW, Weigel D, Poethig RS
evidence.evidence_sentence: miR156 targets 10 members of the SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN LIKE (SPL) family of transcription factors (SPL2, SPL3, SPL4, SPL5, SPL6, SPL9, SPL10, SPL11, SPL13, SPL15).

edges.mechanism: miR156 targets SPL15 transcript

evidence.title: The sequential action of miR156 and miR172 regulates developmental timing in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Wu G, Park MY, Conway SR, Wang JW, Weigel D, Poethig RS
evidence.evidence_sentence: miR156 targets 10 members of the SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN LIKE (SPL) family of transcription factors including SPL15.

edges.mechanism: miR156 targets SPL3 via binding site in 3' UTR

evidence.title: Temporal Regulation of Shoot Development in Arabidopsis Thaliana By miR156 and Its Target SPL3
evidence.authors: Wu G, Poethig RS
evidence.evidence_sentence: SPL3, SPL4 and SPL5 have a target site for the microRNA miR156 in their 3' UTR; GUS expression was absent in the progeny of a cross between transgenic plants carrying 35S::GUS-SPL3 and plants carrying 35S::miR156a.

edges.mechanism: miR156 targets SPL10 transcript

evidence.title: miR156/SPL10 Modulates Lateral Root Development, Branching and Leaf Morphology in Arabidopsis by Silencing AGAMOUS-LIKE 79
evidence.authors: Gao R, Wang Y, Gruber MY, Hannoufa A
evidence.evidence_sentence: miR156OE downregulates SPL10 expression, establishing miR156 repression of SPL10.

edges.mechanism: miR156 targets SPL13 transcript

evidence.title: Developmental Functions of miR156-Regulated SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE (SPL) Genes in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Xu M, Hu T, Zhao J, Park MY, Earley KW, Wu G, Yang L, Poethig RS
evidence.evidence_sentence: miR156 completely blocks the expression of its SPL targets in the first two leaves of the rosette, and represses these genes to different degrees at later stages.

edges.mechanism: SPL9 binds upstream of MIR172B promoter (~1.2 kb upstream) and directly transcriptionally activates miR172b

evidence.title: The sequential action of miR156 and miR172 regulates developmental timing in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Wu G, Park MY, Conway SR, Wang JW, Weigel D, Poethig RS
evidence.evidence_sentence: SPL9 is a direct transcriptional activator of miR172b with enrichment ~5-fold at binding site S1 located 1.2 kb upstream of miR172b transcription start.

edges.mechanism: SPL15 activates MIR172D in shoot apical meristem in short days

evidence.title: Redundant and specific roles of individual MIR172 genes in plant development
evidence.authors: Lian H, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: MIR172D played a critical role in flowering in shoot apical meristems, regulated by SPL15.

edges.mechanism: miR172 targets AP2 transcript

evidence.title: The Critical Role of miRNAs in Regulation of Flowering Time and Flower Development
evidence.authors: Waheed S, Zeng L
evidence.evidence_sentence: In Arabidopsis miR172 negatively regulate the expression of AP2 and five AP2-like genes including TARGET OF EAT 1 (TOE1), TOE2, TOE3, SCHNARCHZAPFEN (SNZ) and SCHLAFMUTZE (SMZ).

edges.mechanism: miR172 targets TOE1 transcript

evidence.title: The Critical Role of miRNAs in Regulation of Flowering Time and Flower Development
evidence.authors: Waheed S, Zeng L
evidence.evidence_sentence: miR172 negatively regulate the expression of AP2 and five AP2-like genes including TARGET OF EAT 1 (TOE1).

edges.mechanism: miR172 targets TOE2 transcript

evidence.title: The Critical Role of miRNAs in Regulation of Flowering Time and Flower Development
evidence.authors: Waheed S, Zeng L
evidence.evidence_sentence: miR172 negatively regulate the expression of AP2 and five AP2-like genes including TOE2.

edges.mechanism: miR172 targets SMZ transcript

evidence.title: The Critical Role of miRNAs in Regulation of Flowering Time and Flower Development
evidence.authors: Waheed S, Zeng L
evidence.evidence_sentence: miR172 negatively regulate the expression of AP2 and five AP2-like genes including SCHLAFMUTZE (SMZ).

edges.mechanism: miR172 targets SNZ transcript

evidence.title: The Critical Role of miRNAs in Regulation of Flowering Time and Flower Development
evidence.authors: Waheed S, Zeng L
evidence.evidence_sentence: miR172 negatively regulate the expression of AP2 and five AP2-like genes including SCHNARCHZAPFEN (SNZ).

edges.mechanism: AP2 is a floral repressor; high miR172 reduces AP2 allowing flowering

evidence.title: Redundant and specific roles of individual MIR172 genes in plant development
evidence.authors: Lian H, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: miR172 contribute to the acquisition of floral competence by silencing these AP2-family factors that otherwise suppress flowering genes.

edges.mechanism: TOE1 is an AP2-like floral repressor

evidence.title: The sequential action of miR156 and miR172 regulates developmental timing in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Wu G, Park MY, Conway SR, Wang JW, Weigel D, Poethig RS
evidence.evidence_sentence: TOE1 OE (35S::TOE1) delays abaxial trichomes 4 plastochrons and causes rounder leaves, while toe1 toe2 loss-of-function accelerates phase change.

edges.mechanism: TOE2 is an AP2-like floral repressor; redundant with TOE1

evidence.title: The sequential action of miR156 and miR172 regulates developmental timing in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Wu G, Park MY, Conway SR, Wang JW, Weigel D, Poethig RS
evidence.evidence_sentence: toe1 toe2 double loss-of-function accelerates abaxial trichomes 3 plastochrons.

edges.mechanism: SMZ is an AP2-like floral repressor

evidence.title: The Critical Role of miRNAs in Regulation of Flowering Time and Flower Development
evidence.authors: Waheed S, Zeng L
evidence.evidence_sentence: toe1 toe2 smz snz quadruple mutant shows early flowering.

edges.mechanism: SNZ is an AP2-like floral repressor

evidence.title: The Critical Role of miRNAs in Regulation of Flowering Time and Flower Development
evidence.authors: Waheed S, Zeng L
evidence.evidence_sentence: toe1 toe2 smz snz quadruple mutant shows early flowering.

edges.mechanism: SPL9 (with SPL2/11/13/15) directly activates FUL expression

evidence.authors: Xu M, Hu T, Zhao J, Park MY, Earley KW, Wu G, Yang L, Poethig RS
evidence.evidence_sentence: spl2/9/11/13/15 mutants showed reduced the abundance of LFY, AP1, FUL, and SOC1 transcripts by about 50%.

edges.mechanism: SPL9 activates SOC1 expression

evidence.authors: Yu S, Galvao VC, Zhang YC, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: ProSPL9:rSPL9 promoted flowering; elevated SOC1 and MIR172b.

evidence.authors: Xu M, Hu T, Zhao J, Park MY, Earley KW, Wu G, Yang L, Poethig RS

evidence.authors: Xu M, Hu T, Zhao J, Park MY, Earley KW, Wu G, Yang L, Poethig RS
evidence.evidence_sentence: spl2/9/11/13/15 mutants showed reduced LFY, AP1, FUL, and SOC1 transcripts.

edges.mechanism: SPL15 activates FUL, especially in short days

evidence.title: Developmental Functions of miR156-Regulated SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE (SPL) Genes in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Xu M, Hu T, Zhao J, Park MY, Earley KW, Wu G, Yang L, Poethig RS
evidence.evidence_sentence: spl2/9/11/13/15 mutants showed reduced LFY, AP1, FUL, and SOC1 transcripts by about 50%.

evidence.authors: Yu S, Galvao VC, Zhang YC, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: SPLs activate SOC1 at SAM; PAC suppressed SOC1, FUL, and MIR172b in WT but not ProSPL9:rSPL9.

edges.mechanism: SPL3 binds and activates LFY promoter

evidence.title: Developmental Functions of miR156-Regulated SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE (SPL) Genes in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Xu M, Hu T, Zhao J, Park MY, Earley KW, Wu G, Yang L, Poethig RS
evidence.evidence_sentence: SPL3 and SPL9 in vivo and are up-regulated in plants over-expressing these genes target LFY and AP1; in spl3/4/5 mutants, expression of LFY and AP1 reduced.

edges.mechanism: SPL3 activates AP1 (floral meristem identity)

evidence.title: Developmental Functions of miR156-Regulated SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE (SPL) Genes in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Xu M, Hu T, Zhao J, Park MY, Earley KW, Wu G, Yang L, Poethig RS
evidence.evidence_sentence: in spl3/4/5 mutants, expression of LFY and AP1 reduced.

edges.mechanism: SPL3 promotes floral induction; miR156-insensitive SPL3 causes early flowering

evidence.title: Temporal Regulation of Shoot Development in Arabidopsis Thaliana By miR156 and Its Target SPL3
evidence.authors: Wu G, Poethig RS
evidence.evidence_sentence: plants expressing SPL3 with mutated miR156 target site (35S::SPL3m) flowered early, had a significantly reduced number of juvenile, adult and cauline leaves.

edges.mechanism: SPL10 directly binds core SPL binding sequences in AGL79 promoter

evidence.title: miR156/SPL10 Modulates Lateral Root Development, Branching and Leaf Morphology in Arabidopsis by Silencing AGAMOUS-LIKE 79
evidence.authors: Gao R, Wang Y, Gruber MY, Hannoufa A
evidence.evidence_sentence: SPL10 was found to bind to the core consensus SPL binding sequences in AGL79 gene; strong binding capacity of SPL10 to regions I, II and III was detected by ChIP-qPCR.

edges.mechanism: DELLA proteins (RGA, GAI) directly bind SPL9 and interfere with SPL9 transcriptional activity

evidence.title: Gibberellin Regulates the Arabidopsis Floral Transition through miR156-Targeted SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING-LIKE Transcription Factors
evidence.authors: Yu S, Galvao VC, Zhang YC, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: DELLA directly binds to microRNA156 (miR156)-targeted SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING-LIKE (SPL) transcription factors; the interaction between DELLA and SPL interferes with SPL transcriptional activity.

edges.mechanism: DELLA proteins directly bind SPL15 and interfere with its activity

evidence.title: Gibberellin Regulates the Arabidopsis Floral Transition through miR156-Targeted SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING-LIKE Transcription Factors
evidence.authors: Yu S, Galvao VC, Zhang YC, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: DELLA directly binds to miR156-targeted SPL transcription factors; the interaction between DELLA and SPL interferes with SPL transcriptional activity.

edges.mechanism: GA promotes DELLA degradation via 17-aa DELLA motif and SCF(SLY1) ubiquitination

evidence.title: Gibberellin Regulates the Arabidopsis Floral Transition through miR156-Targeted SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING-LIKE Transcription Factors
evidence.authors: Yu S, Galvao VC, Zhang YC, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: GAs regulate gene expression by promoting the degradation of the transcriptional regulator DELLA proteins; degradation is mediated by 17 amino acids, called the DELLA motif.

edges.mechanism: Sugar represses MIR156A and MIR156C expression to promote vegetative phase change

evidence.title: A Regulatory Network for miR156-SPL Module in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Zheng C, Ye M, Sang M, Wu R
evidence.evidence_sentence: Sugar promotes vegetative phase change in Arabidopsis thaliana by repressing the expression of MIR156A and MIR156C.

edges.mechanism: AHL15 directly represses SPL9 (and SPL2/13/15) in an miR156-independent manner

evidence.title: miR156-independent repression of the ageing pathway by longevity-promoting AHL proteins in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Rahimi A, Karami O, Balazadeh S, Offringa R
evidence.evidence_sentence: AHL15 and its close homologue AHL20 interfere with the ageing pathway by repressing SPL2/9/13/15 gene expression in an miRNA-independent manner.

edges.mechanism: AHL15 represses SPL15 independently of miR156

evidence.title: miR156-independent repression of the ageing pathway by longevity-promoting AHL proteins in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Rahimi A, Karami O, Balazadeh S, Offringa R
evidence.evidence_sentence: AHL15 and AHL20 interfere with the ageing pathway by repressing SPL2/9/13/15 gene expression in an miRNA-independent manner.

edges.mechanism: SPLs repress AHL15 expression forming reciprocal negative feedback loop

evidence.title: miR156-independent repression of the ageing pathway by longevity-promoting AHL proteins in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Rahimi A, Karami O, Balazadeh S, Offringa R
evidence.evidence_sentence: SPLs promote reproductive identity of AMs by repressing AHL15 expression.

edges.mechanism: miR172 silences AP2/TOE/SMZ/SNZ floral repressors, promoting flowering

evidence.title: Redundant and specific roles of individual MIR172 genes in plant development
evidence.authors: Lian H, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: high amounts of miR172 contribute to the acquisition of floral competence by silencing these AP2-family factors that otherwise suppress flowering genes.

edges.mechanism: miR156 represses SPL transcription factors needed for flowering; high miR156 = juvenile stage; miR156 overexpression delays flowering

evidence.title: Characterizing the Role of the miR156-SPL Network in Plant Development and Stress Response
evidence.authors: Jeyakumar JMJ, Ali A, Wang WM, Thiruvengadam M
evidence.evidence_sentence: miR156 overexpression results in prolonged juvenile and delayed flowering; miR156 knockout significantly shortens juvenile phase.

edges.mechanism: SPL9 activates miR172, FUL, SOC1, AP1, LFY to promote flowering

evidence.title: Developmental Functions of miR156-Regulated SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE (SPL) Genes in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Xu M, Hu T, Zhao J, Park MY, Earley KW, Wu G, Yang L, Poethig RS
evidence.evidence_sentence: SPL2, SPL9, SPL10, SPL11, SPL13, and SPL15 all promote floral induction, and together explain the effect of 35S::MIR156A on this process.

edges.mechanism: SPL15 activates miR172 and SOC1/FUL/AP1 at SAM particularly in short days to promote flowering

evidence.title: Developmental Functions of miR156-Regulated SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE (SPL) Genes in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Xu M, Hu T, Zhao J, Park MY, Earley KW, Wu G, Yang L, Poethig RS
evidence.evidence_sentence: SPL2, SPL9, SPL10, SPL11, SPL13, and SPL15 all promote floral induction.

edges.mechanism: FUL is a MADS-box floral integrator promoting flowering

evidence.title: Gibberellin Regulates the Arabidopsis Floral Transition through miR156-Targeted SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING-LIKE Transcription Factors
evidence.authors: Yu S, Galvao VC, Zhang YC, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: PAC suppressed SOC1, FUL, and MIR172b in WT but not ProSPL9:rSPL9; SPL-FUL cascade promotes flowering.

edges.mechanism: LFY is a floral meristem identity gene promoting flower formation

evidence.title: Developmental Functions of miR156-Regulated SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE (SPL) Genes in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Xu M, Hu T, Zhao J, Park MY, Earley KW, Wu G, Yang L, Poethig RS
evidence.evidence_sentence: spl2/9/11/13/15 mutants showed reduced LFY, AP1, FUL, and SOC1 transcripts; LFY required for floral induction.

edges.mechanism: AP1 is a floral meristem identity gene promoting flowering

evidence.title: Developmental Functions of miR156-Regulated SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE (SPL) Genes in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Xu M, Hu T, Zhao J, Park MY, Earley KW, Wu G, Yang L, Poethig RS
evidence.evidence_sentence: spl2/9/11/13/15 mutants showed reduced LFY, AP1, FUL, and SOC1 transcripts; AP1 specifies floral meristem identity.

edges.mechanism: SOC1 is a MADS-box floral integrator activating LFY and AP1

evidence.title: Gibberellin Regulates the Arabidopsis Floral Transition through miR156-Targeted SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING-LIKE Transcription Factors
evidence.authors: Yu S, Galvao VC, Zhang YC, et al.
evidence.evidence_sentence: SPLs activate miR172 and MADS box genes controlling flowering; SOC1 promotes flowering.

edges.mechanism: CLF PRC2 component catalyzes H3K27me3 at FLC; loss of CLF increases FLC

evidence.title: Autonomous Pathway: FLOWERING LOCUS C Repression through an Antisense-Mediated Chromatin-Silencing Mechanism
evidence.authors: Wu Z, Fang X, Zhu D, Dean C
evidence.evidence_sentence: Loss of CLF leads to reduction of H3K27me3 genome wide, accompanied by increased FLC expression although to a lesser extent than that observed in fca or fpa mutants.

edges.mechanism: SPL9 feedback represses miR156 (negative feedback loop reinforcing phase change)
